finding_results.notes: The opening now establishes a single Crook roster on-page: Thessa, Petra, Ruald, Denna, Kael, and Hale. The evacuation order keeps those same people in motion, and Thessa's casualty report plus Maren's accounting resolves the full alive/dead math without introducing stray names or uncertain survivors.

The bowl is framed as a carried habit-object rather than an active ritual instrument. Maren explicitly is not using it during practice, drops it because it is in the way before the nexus opening, cracks it incidentally under his knee, and leaves with it still only as a damaged possession. The fake-to-real bowl turn is no longer spent here.

finding_results.notes: Caul's break now lands in the ridge exchange and his pivot into defense. The later section is compressed so each subsequent paragraph either changes the escape situation, clarifies who survived, forces the retreat choice, or alters the Maren/Thessa relationship around blame and cost.
Revised em-dash density is 1 em-dash line out of 112 prose lines (0.9%). The remaining em dash is a genuine live interruption in dialogue rather than an expository default.

summary: Chapter 15 now uses a reconciled Crook roster, preserves the bowl's Chapter 16 payoff, trims the back third so Caul's break is not re-explained, and cuts em-dash density from packet-failing levels to a single interruption line.
